revision.text:
{{nihongo|'''The Yaizu Shrine'''|焼津神社|Yaizu Jinja}}, also called {{nihongo|'''Irie Daimyōjin'''|入江大明神}}, is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Yaizu, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]].
== Enshrined deities == * [[Yamatotakeru]] * [[Kibi no Takehiko]] * [[Otomo no Takehi]] * [[Nanatsukahagi]]
== History and Legend == According to [[Fudoki|''Suruga-no-Kuni Fudoki'']], the Yaizu Shrine was established in [[409]]. [[Yamatotakeru]] was trapped and almost burnt to death when he was on a journey around Yaizu. However, he succeeded in escaping from the fire by swinging the magical [[Kusanagi Sword]]. To memorize this event, the shrine was built.
== Festivals == * Aramatsuri (annually on August 12 - 13)
== See also == * [[Atsuta Shrine]]
== References == * Shizuokaken Kyodokenkyukai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980
[[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{shinto-stub}}
[[ja:焼津神社]]

== See also == * [[Atsuta Shrine]]
== References == * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980

[[ja:焼津神社]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Yaizu Jinja
焼津神社 | image = Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja cr.jpg | caption = Statue of Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja | type = | dedication = Yamato Takeru no Mikoto and 3 followers | founded = | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = 2-7-1 Yaizu-chō, Yaizu, Shizuoka, 425-0026 | phone = 054-628-2444 | website = http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja/7001.html }} {{nihongo|'''Yaizu Jinja'''|焼津神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Yaizu, Shizuoka|Yaizu]] in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|Yamato Takeru|日本武尊}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru’s miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja’s foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] b the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12 - 13) is the shrine’s main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{coord|34|51|54|N|138|18|49|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[ja: 焼津神社]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Yaizu Jinja
焼津神社 | image = Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja cr.jpg | caption = Statue of Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|54|N|138|18|49|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Yamato Takeru no Mikoto and 3 followers | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 2-7-1 Yaizu-chō, Yaizu, Shizuoka, 425-0026 | website = {{URL|http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja/7001.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yaizu Jinja'''|焼津神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Yaizu, Shizuoka|Yaizu]] in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru’s miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja’s foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] b the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12 - 13) is the shrine’s main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== {{commonscat|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Yaizu Jinja
焼津神社 | image = Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja cr.jpg | caption = Statue of Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|54|N|138|18|49|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Yamato Takeru no Mikoto and 3 followers | founded_by = | established = c.409 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 2-7-1 Yaizu-chō, Yaizu, Shizuoka, 425-0026 | website = {{Official|1=https://yaizujinja.or.jp}} | architecture_style = ''[[Nagare-zukuri]]'' | festival = August 13 | leadership = }} [[file:Yaizu-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|right|270px|''Haiden'' of Yaizu Jinja]] {{nihongo|'''Yaizu Jinja'''|焼津神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Yaizu, Shizuoka|Yaizu]] in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru’s miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja’s foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12 - 13) is the shrine’s main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Yaizu Jinja
焼津神社 | image = Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja cr.jpg | caption = Statue of Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|54|N|138|18|49|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Yamato Takeru no Mikoto and 3 followers | founded_by = | established = c.409 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 2-7-1 Yaizu-chō, Yaizu, Shizuoka, 425-0026 | website = {{Official website|1=https://yaizujinja.or.jp}} | architecture_style = ''[[Nagare-zukuri]]'' | festival = August 13 | leadership = }} [[file:Yaizu-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|right|270px|''Haiden'' of Yaizu Jinja]] {{nihongo|'''Yaizu Jinja'''|焼津神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Yaizu, Shizuoka|Yaizu]] in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]]

His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia35]]

It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Yaizu Jinja
焼津神社 | image = Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja cr.jpg | caption = Statue of Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|54|N|138|18|49|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Yamato Takeru no Mikoto and 3 followers | founded_by = | established = c.409 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 2-7-1 Yaizu-chō, Yaizu, Shizuoka, 425-0026 | website = {{Official website|1=https://yaizujinja.or.jp}} | architecture_style = ''[[Nagare-zukuri]]'' | festival = August 13 | leadership = }} [[file:Yaizu-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|right|270px|''Haiden'' of Yaizu Jinja]] {{nihongo|'''Yaizu Jinja'''|焼津神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Yaizu, Shizuoka|Yaizu]] in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:mm]][[category:mm]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Yaizu Jinja
焼津神社 | image = Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja cr.jpg | caption = Statue of Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|54|N|138|18|49|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Yamato Takeru no Mikoto and 3 followers | founded_by = | established = c.409 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 2-7-1 Yaizu-chō, Yaizu, Shizuoka, 425-0026 | website = {{Official website|1=https://yaizujinja.or.jp}} | architecture_style = ''[[Nagare-zukuri]]'' | festival = August 13 | leadership = }} [[file:Yaizu-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|right|270px|''Haiden'' of Yaizu Jinja]] {{nihongo|'''Yaizu Jinja'''|焼津神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Yaizu, Shizuoka|Yaizu]] in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]][[category:mm]] [[category:qq]] [[category:ファイル]]

The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Yaizu Jinja
焼津神社 | image = Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja cr.jpg | caption = Statue of Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|54|N|138|18|49|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Yamato Takeru no Mikoto and 3 followers | founded_by = | established = c.409 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 2-7-1 Yaizu-chō, Yaizu, Shizuoka, 425-0026 | website = {{Official website|1=https://yaizujinja.or.jp}} | architecture_style = ''[[Nagare-zukuri]]'' | festival = August 13 | leadership = }} [[file:Yaizu-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|right|270px|''Haiden'' of Yaizu Jinja]] {{nihongo|'''Yaizu Jinja'''|焼津神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Yaizu, Shizuoka|Yaizu]] in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]]

The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]][[category:mm]] [[category:dd]][[category:Yaizu Shrine]][[category:dd]] [[category:1 revision imported]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[category:Yaizu Shrine]][[category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[category:refreshing imports]] [[category:kk]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. 焼津神社 | image = Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja cr.jpg | caption = Statue of Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|54|N|138|18|49|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Yamato Takeru no Mikoto and 3 followers | founded_by = | established = c.409 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 2-7-1 Yaizu-chō, Yaizu, Shizuoka, 425-0026 | website = {{Official website|1=https://yaizujinja.or.jp}} | architecture_style = ''[[Nagare-zukuri]]'' | festival = August 13 | leadership = }} [[file:Yaizu-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|right|270px|''Haiden'' of Yaizu Jinja]] {{nihongo|'''Yaizu Jinja'''|焼津神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Yaizu, Shizuoka|Yaizu]] in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:qq 1]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 1]]\[[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:qq 1]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Cqq1]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 1]]\[[Category:Cqq1]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to cqq5]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to cqq5]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Yaizu Jinja
焼津神社 | image = Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja cr.jpg | caption = Statue of Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|54|N|138|18|49|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Yamato Takeru no Mikoto and 3 followers | founded_by = | established = c.409 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 2-7-1 Yaizu-chō, Yaizu, Shizuoka, 425-0026 | website = {{Official website|1=https://yaizujinja.or.jp}} | architecture_style = ''[[Nagare-zukuri]]'' | festival = August 13 | leadership = }} [[file:Yaizu-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|right|270px|''Haiden'' of Yaizu Jinja]] {{nihongo|'''Yaizu Jinja'''|焼津神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Yaizu, Shizuoka|Yaizu]] in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:11 1]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 5]][[Category:11 1]]\[[Category:C111]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:11 1]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11 1]]\[[Category:C111]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Yaizu Jinja
焼津神社 | image = Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja cr.jpg | caption = Statue of Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|54|N|138|18|49|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Yamato Takeru no Mikoto and 3 followers | founded_by = | established = c.409 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 2-7-1 Yaizu-chō, Yaizu, Shizuoka, 425-0026 | website = {{Official website|1=https://yaizujinja.or.jp}} | architecture_style = ''[[Nagare-zukuri]]'' | festival = August 13 | leadership = }} [[file:Yaizu-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|right|270px|''Haiden'' of Yaizu Jinja]] {{nihongo|'''Yaizu Jinja'''|焼津神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Yaizu, Shizuoka|Yaizu]] in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] \[[Category:C111]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] \[[Category:C111]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Yaizu Jinja
焼津神社 | image = Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja cr.jpg | caption = Statue of Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|54|N|138|18|49|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Yamato Takeru no Mikoto and 3 followers | founded_by = | established = c.409 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 2-7-1 Yaizu-chō, Yaizu, Shizuoka, 425-0026 | website = {{Official website|1=https://yaizujinja.or.jp}} | architecture_style = ''[[Nagare-zukuri]]'' | festival = August 13 | leadership = }} [[file:Yaizu-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|right|270px|''Haiden'' of Yaizu Jinja]] {{nihongo|'''Yaizu Jinja'''|焼津神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Yaizu, Shizuoka|Yaizu]] in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons Category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]][[Category:mm]] [[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] \[[Category:C111]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Yaizu Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[sn:Yaizu Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Yaizu Jinja
焼津神社 | image = Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja cr.jpg | caption = Statue of Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|54|N|138|18|49|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Yamato Takeru no Mikoto and 3 followers | founded_by = | established = c.409 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 2-7-1 Yaizu-chō, Yaizu, Shizuoka, 425-0026 | website = {{Official website|1=https://yaizujinja.or.jp}} | architecture_style = ''[[Nagare-zukuri]]'' | festival = August 13 | leadership = }} [[file:Yaizu-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|right|270px|''Haiden'' of Yaizu Jinja]] {{nihongo|'''Yaizu Jinja'''|焼津神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Yaizu, Shizuoka|Yaizu]] in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yaizu Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Yaizu Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[sn:Yaizu Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Yaizu Jinja
焼津神社 | image = Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja cr.jpg | caption = Statue of Yamato Takeru at Yaizu Jinja | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|54|N|138|18|49|E|region:JP_scale:30000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Yamato Takeru no Mikoto and 3 followers | founded_by = | established = c.409 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 2-7-1 Yaizu-chō, Yaizu, Shizuoka, 425-0026 | website = {{Official website|1=https://yaizujinja.or.jp}} | architecture_style = ''[[Nagare-zukuri]]'' | festival = August 13 | leadership = }} [[file:Yaizu-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|right|270px|''Haiden'' of Yaizu Jinja]] {{nihongo|'''Yaizu Jinja'''|焼津神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[Yaizu, Shizuoka|Yaizu]] in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Yaizu, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yaizu Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Yaizu Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yaizu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[sn:Yaizu Shrine]]

The shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|Irie Daimyojin|入江大明神|}}.The main festival of the shrine is annually on August 13. ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Yaizu is the {{nihongo|[[Yamato Takeru]]|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, the legendary 4th century AD prince of the [[Yamato dynasty]], son of [[Emperor Keikō]], whose legends are detailed in the Japanese chronicles ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Yaizu Jinja claims to have the remaining stone, as well as to have been built on the location of Yamato Takeru's miraculous escape. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. Per one of the legends in the Yamato Takeru cycle, Emperor Keikō sent him to the eastern provinces, whose ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' had rebelled against the imperial court. His enemies attempted to kill him by setting fire to a grassy plain, but he escaped by using the powers of his magical sword, the ''[[Kusanagi no Tsurugi|Kusanagi no tsurugi]]'' and two magic stones. The sword, one of the [[Imperial Regalia of Japan]] is kept at [[Atsuta Shrine]] in [[Nagoya]], along with one of the magic stones. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[:en:Koku|Koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] }} [[file:Yaizu-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|right|270px|''Haiden'' of Yaizu Jinja]] [[sn:Yaizu Shrine]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Yaizu-jinja}} [[fr:Yaizu-jinja]] [[ja:焼津神社]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]]

The remaining three ''kami'' enshrined at Yaizu Jinja are the three main retainers of Yamato Takeru during his voyage east, including his brother-in-law {{nihongo|Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|吉備武彦命|}}, {{nihongo|Otomo no Takehi no Mikoto|大伴武日連命|}} and {{nihongo|Nanatsukahagi no Mikoto|七束脛命|}} Yaizu Jinja also has numerous small subsidiary shrines dedicated to various ''kami'' within its precincts. ==History== The date of Yaizu Jinja's foundation is unknown. Per shrine tradition it was founded in 409 AD. In the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] ''[[Engishiki]]'' records dated 927 AD, the shrine is mentioned as one of four shrines in Mashizu District of [[Suruga Province]]. The shrine was patronized by the [[Imagawa clan]] in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], and given revenues of 500 ''[[:en:Koku|Koku]]'' for its upkeep. This was reduced to a 70 ''koku'' stipend in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] by the [[Tokugawa shogunate]]. In 1883, Yaizu Jinja was recognized as a “Prefectural Shrine” under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] within the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] system. == Festivals == * ''Aramatsuri'' (annually on August 12–13) is the shrine's main festival. It involves a procession with two ''[[mikoshi]]'' and is considered one of the roughest festivals in the [[Tōkai region]]. ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] ==References== * Shizuokaken Kyōdokenkyūkai, ''Shizuokaken Jinja Shi'', 1980 {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Yaizu-jinja|Yaizu Jinja}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] }} [[file:Yaizu-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|right|270px|''Haiden'' of Yaizu Jinja]] [[sn:Yaizu Shrine]] [[en:Yaizu Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Yaizu-jinja}} [[fr:Yaizu-jinja]] [[ja:焼津神社]]
